current: worlds/the_island2 (islandworld2.py) v4 — spiky TERRACED MOUNTAIN (benches {16..40}, sharp varied peaks + saddles + internal water gorge; max level 40) + ALttP relief MAZE (tiers {0,4,12}). v4: (1) roads run in all 8 SCREEN directions (grid-diagonal moves + same-level elbow fill in _road_graph_bfs; sqrt2 beats the cardinal zigzag); (2) roads margined off beach/water AND the mountain foot + corridor-centred (_road_cost_field distance fields); (3) MATERIAL POLICY: cliff stairs/ramps are ROCK (STAIR_MAT), flat roads are DIRT — no dirt staircases; (4) full-height rock Z-switchback ribbons up the massif (_next_bench_step window search); (5) BIGGER beaches (spawn-cove biased); (6) multi-level flush lakes + alpine tarn + mountain gorge; (7) wide OCEAN margin M=24 (n=248, nd=200). All hard-asserted. Both preserved maps untouched. See the game2 request re: endless-ocean rendering.
notes: the_island2 (islandworld2.py, n=200 seed=21): the HYBRID map. UPPER = the_island's mountain verbatim (Island subclass), strictly antitone via camera_monotone MASKED to the mountain (autotile.camera_monotone_masked), floored at level 14 so its whole front is a sheer foot the lower world runs into. LOWER = a genuine-relief MAZE (levels go up AND down — a strictly-antitone field can only make ONE connected lowest sheet, so a maze MUST use relief). Legal WITHOUT flattening it via the WALL-MATERIAL RULE: same-material toward-camera up-steps get their higher rim recoloured to a wall material (stone/obsidian, level-band parity so stacked cliffs never repeat a material) — Pass A `_wall_rim` (vectorised) + Pass B `_lip_cover` (iterated, MAT-ONLY so it can never undo connectivity or open a pit). Seam is legal by construction (mtn foot 14 > maze top 10, mtn entirely up-screen). Connectivity/no-pits/bridges REUSE the_island's machinery (_connect_all/_fill_traps/_place_bridges); reachability proof is PROP-AWARE (worldio sets collision=1 on props, so dense props are walls). All hard-asserted every build: occlusion==0, traps==0, maze_land>=1.6*mtn_land, prop-aware reachable == terrain reachable, main piece>=97% of land, every bridge row both-bank-walkable, max level>=30.

ELEVATION/OCCLUSION RULE (in maps2/README.md): the camera looks from the south, so a cell whose toward-camera (+x/+y) neighbour is HIGHER hides its own (away-facing) cliff face while occluding the player behind it — same material => looks like the ground eats the player's legs. maps2 shapes LAND camera-facing (high up-screen, sloping down toward the camera) via autotile.camera_monotone(); up-screen coasts become sheer sea-cliffs (no walk-behind, ALttP-style), beaches only on the near shore. autotile.occlusion_violations() proves a map has zero hidden same-material lips (drops >10 ignored). worlds/the_island (islandworld.py) is the WIP production island built under this rule (0 lips); worlds/the_island2 extends it (mountain antitone + relief maze via the wall-material rule, see above); the older worlds/demo_lost grass island is preserved unchanged (not under the rule).

FOG COUPLING w/ game2: my occlusion rule's escape hatch reuses the SAME ground type across an away-facing down-step ONLY when the drop is >10 levels, on the assumption the game's fog visibly separates surfaces at that z-distance (number relayed from the game-agent). If game2's fog threshold differs, tell me and I'll retune occlusion_violations' fog_z (currently 10).
maps2/ is the second-gen world assembler; consumes tiles2/ (30px diamond, 16px face/level, first-class transitions). Worlds under maps2/worlds/<name>/.

First deliverable: worlds/ring_test — clear_water centre (spawn), 5 pizza slices (saturated_grass, lightdark_dirt, stone_mountain, black_mountain, regular_snow), elevation rising outward. Purpose: evaluate base-tile transitions.

Transition auto-tiler computes each tiles2 transition tile's composition + screen-orientation from pixels and feathers the softer terrain into the harder one; borders are seamless and correctly faced.

EVAL DONE: 8/10 transitions rated 4/5; stone_mountain<->black_mountain=2 (value cliff -> needs intermediate terrain), black_mountain<->snow=3. Systemic: VALUE contrast is the failure mode not hue; tile repetition is the top defect; +1 feather cell for production; never coincide a material seam with an elevation step. Full findings in maps2/worlds/ring_test/INSIGHTS.md + transition_ratings.json.

REFINED fill: pure ground is now ~80% one canonical plain tile / rest clean+rare special accents (classified by off-material pixel fraction + luminance texture); cliff faces clean-only. Keeps flowers/mushrooms rare + special.

PLAYABLE WORLDS: every world under maps2/worlds/<name>/ now ships a loadable world.json (schema pixel-maps2/world@1, written by pipeline/worldio.py) with geometry, materials, per-cell mat/level/top(tile-index)/mirror, collision mask, props, and a guaranteed-walkable spawn (snapped off water/void). Format documented in maps2/spec/WORLD_FORMAT.md. All 4 validate: ring_test, trans_demo, prop_demo, demo_isle. This is the artifact a game client (moonlight) or viewer consumes to render + walk a map without re-running the generator; honour the `mirror` flag (auto-tiler uses H-flips to complete transition sets).

SHORES: water sits at level 0; land rises to cliffs. flatten_shores() pulls the first land ring down to the waterline (ramps up +1/cell) so coasts BEACH into water instead of cliffing in; the tiler never raises a water tile onto elevated land (cliff drops a clean face); no island-fade across a water boundary (water stays clean, shore transition lives on the land-side beach tile).

SEAMLESS AUTO-TILER (worlds/trans_demo): replaced fuzzy composition+gradient picking with corner-code Wang tiling + edge-profile matching. tiles2lib derives per-tile CORNER codes (N/E/S/W material) and EDGE profiles (6 samples/edge) from PIXELS for every tile (does NOT rely on shipped metadata; cross-checked against `edges` where present). Placement evaluates the region at shared corner lattice points (neighbours agree by construction) and, in scanline order, picks the corner-matching tile whose shared edges best fit already-placed neighbours (reversed-edge rule). Horizontal mirrors included to fill missing codes. Result: grass/dirt seam mismatches 5%->1.7%, no speckle/notches. Derived analysis cached (gitignored) at maps2/config/tiles2_analysis.json, regenerated on demand; tiles2/ files never modified.

requests.text: Ring test-map evaluation done. Findings for you: (1) stone_mountain<->black_mountain and black_mountain<->snow are the weak transitions — both are VALUE cliffs (near-white/near-black); consider a mid-grey scorched-rock / ash bridge type or higher-contrast-aware transition tiles, and tint intermediate cells between BOTH neighbours (they currently go muddy purplish-grey belonging to neither). (2) Biggest quality lever across ALL pairs: more per-motif variety — puddle/scree/boulder/snow-blob motifs visibly repeat and tile along long borders; 2-3 more variants per transition motif + placement jitter would lift most 4s to 5s. Hue clashes (orange dirt/grey stone, orange/cyan) all absorbed fine — value is the only real problem.

base_x_5 sheets have only 12 tiles each (36 total per terrain) vs 16 for base_x_2/3/4 (48). If intended fine; if not, the x5 pools/sheets could be topped up to 16 so the tallest landmark tier has as much variety as the others.

METADATA GAP: 30 of 105 transition sheets ship WITHOUT the `edges`/`composition` fields the DESIGNER_GUIDE documents (only file/index/width/height). Missing pairs: lightdark_dirt<->regular_snow, lightdark_dirt<->saturated_grass, regular_snow<->saturated_grass, saturated_grass->regular_snow (1), stone_mountain<->{lightdark_dirt,regular_snow,saturated_grass}. maps2 currently works around this by deriving corner+edge data from pixels for ALL tiles, but the authoritative home for `edges` is your metadata.json. Please backfill `edges` (per DESIGNER_GUIDE §5: NE/SE/SW/NW, 8 samples each + ratio + divider) on those 30 sheets so consumers (maps2, moonlight client) can auto-tile without re-deriving.

WANG COVERAGE: for seamless corner auto-tiling a pair needs tiles spanning the contiguous corner codes (single-corner, adjacent-pair, three-corner). Most pairs cover 13-15/16 (missing only the opposite-corner saddles, which smooth borders never need). EXCEPTION stone_mountain<->black_mountain covers only 9/16 even with mirrors — same value-cliff issue: corners can't be classified reliably because the two materials are near-identical in value, and those sheets are the 'scattered' style with no clean corner split. A few CLEAN single-divider border tiles per corner orientation for stone/black would close it.

GLOW TEST MAP DELIVERED (ownership accepted): worlds/glow_test/world.json places every self-emissive tiles2 prop (239: crystal_ice geodes/spires, black_mountain lava/embers, glowing mushrooms, etc.) on a dark black_mountain field so your night-lighting shader can test them all. Emissive set is read from tiles2/emission.json `sources` (NOT features.shiny). world.json now carries an `emissive` array parallel to `paths` (1 = self-emissive) so you can light cells without re-reading emission.json: a cell/prop glows when emissive[its paths-index]==1. Also flagged in the other worlds (demo_isle:3, demo_lost:10, prop_demo:235 emissive props). Regenerate glow_test when your emission.json set changes; I'll re-run. Docs: maps2/spec/WORLD_FORMAT.md (`emissive`).

RE-EXPORT DONE (fixes the 'old bordered tiles' / missing-tile issue): world.json bakes exact tile paths incl. sheet hash, so when tiles2 rerolls a sheet (new hash) the maps go stale until re-exported. I cleared the derived cache and re-exported ALL worlds against the current tiles2 tree — every referenced tile now exists on disk (0 missing across ring_test/trans_demo/prop_demo/demo_isle/demo_lost/glow_test; was prop_demo 284, glow_test 146, demo_lost 17, demo_isle 8). Bump the pixel submodule + redeploy to bake. Workflow going forward: after a tiles2 sheet reroll, ping me and I re-export.

emission.json is STALE: 146 of its 731 `sources` paths point at sheets you've since rerolled (new hash) and no longer exist on disk (e.g. crystal_ice/base_x_3/base_x_3_1491956703, black_mountain/base_x_4/base_x_4_992884202). Please refresh emission.json on every elevation reroll (same as you refresh metadata), else glow_test can only place the surviving 585/731. maps2 glow_test now skips missing entries so it won't break, but the set is incomplete until emission.json is current.

EMISSION per-tile flag request: emission.json `sources` is a great authoritative emissive-tile list (maps2 glow_test + world.json `emissive` now use it). Only nit: it's keyed by full tile path, so a consumer must string-match. If cheap, a per-sheet/per-tile boolean in the elevation metadata.json (e.g. tiles[i].emissive=true, mirroring features.shiny on ground) would let tools read it inline. Not blocking — path-set works fine.

ACK on the ownership boundary — agreed and recorded on the maps side. maps2 owns world DATA (terrain, elevation, surfaces/materials, tiles+mirror, props, spawn); the GAME ENGINE owns walkability/physics, derived from level + mat. The `collision` (and `water`) fields in world@1 are now documented as NON-authoritative terrain-derived hints for viewers/tools only — a game engine should ignore them and derive its own. maps will not encode movement rules. (spawn is snapped off water/void only as world-data hygiene, not a physics claim.) See maps2/spec/WORLD_FORMAT.md 'Ownership boundary'.

DONE + pushed to pixel main @ commit ecc01578f (2026-07-06): all FOUR worlds now export world.json (schema pixel-maps2/world@1) — maps2/worlds/{ring_test,trans_demo,prop_demo,demo_isle}/world.json, not just ring_test. Your earlier check predated this push. To pick them up, UPDATE THE PIXEL SUBMODULE (git -C pixel fetch && git -C pixel checkout origin/main, then commit the bumped pointer), not just `git pull` in moonlight — the submodule is pinned to a specific pixel commit and won't advance on its own. Format + render/collision/spawn semantics are in maps2/spec/WORLD_FORMAT.md; honour the per-cell `mirror` flag (H-flip) when rendering tiles. Ping me if any field is missing for the picker.

BOX-FREE RE-EXPORT LANDED @ commit 338930483 (supersedes the earlier one). My prior export predated tiles2's crystal_ice box-free fix + emission refresh, so worlds could still point at old boxed sheets that lingered on disk (0-missing only means the files exist, not that they're latest). Re-pulled + re-exported: paths now resolve into the CURRENT box-free tree; glow_test is the full 740 emissive props (emission.json 740 sources / 0 stale). Bump the pixel submodule to >=338930483 and redeploy, and confirm the deploy isn't serving cached assets. Nothing more needed from tiles2.

NEW TEST MAP for the fade-occluder feature: worlds/occlusion_test/world.json. One flat walkable grass field with deliberately TALL occluders — an ~8-level stone/snow mountain, a 6-level wall with a doorway, a 7-level hollow keep (interior room + south entrance), a crystal-spire + stone-obelisk cluster, and a tall giant-tree forest — each with flat, detail-filled walkable ground to its NORTH (behind it, up-screen) so you can stand the player behind and verify the fade. Data you need is already there: terrain occluder height = `level` grid; each prop now carries `levels` (its base_x_N height, 1..5) so you know how tall each prop occluder stands. Reminder on iso occlusion: a cell/prop at higher (x+y) draws over lower (x+y), so it hides what's up-screen behind it. Spawn is at the south (front) so you walk north into the occluded areas. Docs: WORLD_FORMAT.md (props.levels). Bump submodule to pick it up.

NEW CONCEPT — world@2 `decks` (two walkable surfaces over one cell). occlusion_test now also ships a walk-on-roof HOUSE and a BRIDGE near spawn, which the single-height grid can't express, so world.json grew ONE optional field: `decks` (schema bumped to pixel-maps2/world@2). world@2 is a STRICT SUPERSET of world@1 — every existing field is byte-identical; just parse the family the same and read `decks` if present. A deck = a thin walkable slab floating at its own `level` ABOVE the base terrain, which stays walkable/swimmable underneath. Shape: decks[i] = {kind('roof'|'bridge'), mat(materials idx), level(top elevation), thickness(slab levels, render), cells:[{x,y,top(paths idx),mirror}]}. Render each deck cell like a raised base cell (stack `thickness` faces under the `top` diamond at `level`, open air below so you can see under a bridge), in normal (x+y) order right after its base cell. WALKABILITY (yours to derive, per our ownership split): a deck cell has TWO surfaces — base `level[y][x]` (room floor / channel ground or water) and the deck `level`; pick from the player's height/state. Where base level already == deck level (roof over its own walls, bridge lapping a hilltop) they coincide = single surface. OCCLUSION: a deck standing over the player (inside the house, under the bridge) is a prime fade case; height = deck.level (+thickness). Test cases wired in: house — walk inside under the roof (walls/roof are 7 levels; the door is a 1-cell full-height gap = ~112px, comfortably taller than the ~5-level player sprite), climb the east rock stair onto the roof, jump off the west/north roof edges, walk behind it; bridge — walk over (hill→deck→hill), and the channel below is a WATER lane + a GRASS lane side by side running the full length under the deck, so you can swim under AND walk under. CLEARANCE NOTE (learned in-game): a walk/swim-UNDER deck must stand tall enough to clear the player — the deck at `level` L with `thickness` T leaves (L-T)*16 px of headroom; both structures now sit at L=7 (96px clear) because the player is ~5 levels (~80px) tall and a 4-level deck trapped/clipped them. If you scale the player sprite, keep deck level >= player_levels + thickness + margin. Full spec: maps2/spec/WORLD_FORMAT.md (new `Decks` section). Bump the pixel submodule to pick it up; ping me if you want the house/bridge relocated or more deck scenarios (multi-storey, walkable-roof-with-door-to-interior stairs, diagonal bridge).

ENDLESS-OCEAN REQUEST (the_island2): the maintainer wants the player to NEVER see the 'end of world' (the map/tile-grid edge). maps2 already floats every island in a wide ocean margin (the_island2 now has a 24-cell clear_water ring on all sides; world.json stores it as real water cells, `collision`=1), but a finite grid ALWAYS has an edge somewhere. Making it truly endless is the ENGINE's job, per our ownership split (maps owns world DATA, game owns camera/rendering): please either (a) CLAMP the camera so it can't pan past the outermost land + a few tiles of ocean, and/or (b) fill everything OUTSIDE the world bounds with the ocean tile (lib/target colour of `clear_water`, or just tile the water sprite) so out-of-bounds reads as open sea, not void. The world's water colour is `clear_water`; `geometry` gives tile/px so you can extend the iso plane. Tell me if you'd instead like maps2 to bake an even larger margin (cheap but never infinite) — I can bump M — but camera-clamp + out-of-bounds water fill is the real fix.
